name: Tetris
description: Tetris is a single-player puzzle game where the playing field consists of a grid, typically taller than it is wide. Pieces, known as tetrominoes, composed of four squares each, fall from the top of the grid. There are seven different tetromino shapes, each composed of a unique arrangement of four squares. The player can rotate and translate these pieces as they fall. The objective is to manipulate the pieces to create horizontal lines of blocks without any gaps. When a complete horizontal line is formed, it disappears, and all blocks above it shift down by one row. The game continues until the stack of blocks reaches the top of the grid, at which point the game ends. The game's speed typically increases as the player progresses, causing the tetrominoes to fall faster. A preview of the next tetromino to fall is usually displayed to the player. Scoring is based on the number of lines cleared, with more points awarded for clearing multiple lines simultaneously.

controls.arrow_left: Move the current tetromino one cell to the left, if possible.
controls.arrow_right: Move the current tetromino one cell to the right, if possible.
controls.arrow_down: Increase the falling speed of the current tetromino.
controls.space: Immediately drop the current tetromino to the lowest possible position within the grid.
controls.c: Hold the current tetromino and swap it with the currently held tetromino. If no tetromino is currently held, hold the current one and the next piece becomes the current one.
